children.name: Semnornis
children.description: A small genus of barbet-like birds that form an evolutionary link between New World barbets and toucans, sharing characteristics of both groups. They have massive, brightly coloured bills like toucans but the stout body and bristled bill base of barbets. They inhabit montane forests of Central and South America.

children.name: Semnornis frantzii
children.description: Found in the highland forests of Costa Rica and western Panama, this striking bird has a yellow and black bill with a distinctive prong-like tip, a grey head, and olive-green body. It is a specialised frugivore that follows fruiting trees in mixed-species flocks. Its call is a loud, frog-like croak.

children.name: Semnornis ramphastinus
children.description: Endemic to the cloud forests of the western Andes of Colombia and Ecuador, this is one of the most spectacular birds of the region. It has a grey head with a yellow throat, a bright red crescent on the lower breast, and an enormous yellow-and-black bill with a dark tip. It is threatened by habitat loss and trapping for the pet trade.
